differentials.name: a number of immature primordial follicles begin to mature in response to FSH
differentials.raw: a number of immature primordial follicles begin to mature in response to FSH

differentials.name: 2-3 follicles capture the most FSH and aromatize the most estradiol from their granulosa cells
differentials.raw: 2-3 follicles capture the most FSH and aromatize the most estradiol from their granulosa cells

differentials.name: "Mittelschmerz" = pain just prior to ovulation
differentials.raw: "Mittelschmerz" = pain just prior to ovulation (pressure of graafian follicle distending ovarian capsule)

differentials.name: LUTEAL PHASE = days 15-28 =  remnant dominant follicle transforms into corpus luteum hemorrhagicum
differentials.raw: LUTEAL PHASE = days 15-28 =  remnant dominant follicle transforms into corpus luteum hemorrhagicum

differentials.name: rapidly rising estradiol production triggers the hypothalamic arcuate nucleus to increase
differentials.raw: rapidly rising estradiol production triggers the hypothalamic arcuate nucleus to increase

differentials.name: LH binds to ovarian receptors → releases cAMP from granulosa cells → halting granulosa cell mitosis + increasing peptidase, collagenase, growth factor, angiotensin, prostaglandin as the cause for follicular rupture + conversion to corpus luteum
differentials.raw: LH binds to ovarian receptors → releases cAMP from granulosa cells → halting granulosa cell mitosis + increasing peptidase, collagenase, growth factor, angiotensin, prostaglandin as the cause for follicular rupture + conversion to corpus luteum

context: √  high-impedance waveform √  high-impedance waveform with RI close to 1.0 ›  days 7-22 = midfollicular to midluteal phase =  developing dominant follicle + ovulation + corpus luteal phase: √  continuous diastolic flow with RI close to 0.5 ›  days 23-28 = late luteal phase: √  high-impedance waveform with RI close to 1.0 Ovarian Cycle √  multiple small cysts: (a)  unstimulated follicles are < 2 mm in size (b)  stimulated follicles grow > 2 mm in size √  2-3 follicles in each ovary of day 4 enlarge subsequently to ~ 10 mm √  follicles may be seen by 5-7 days √  single more ascendant / dominant
